hypothesis: In a two-objective setting (minimize formation energy per atom to ≤−3.8 eV/atom while targeting a band gap of 1–2 eV), replacing scalarized numeric rewards with natural-language Pareto-dominance feedback in MatAgent will raise the V.U.N. score by at least 0.20 and increase the number of distinct elements explored by at least 30% over 20 independent 15-iteration runs with identical initialization and compute budgets.
